subject: [PATCH 0/2] fuse: Fix possible memleak at startup with immediate teardown

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch's placement of kfree_ring_ent() in fuse_uring_create() is before the increase of queue_refs, which may not be sufficient to prevent a hang; suggested moving it after the increase

Reviewer noted that the original patch does not address a registration vs abort race, which causes the umount thread to hang in TASK_UNINTERRUPTIBLE state even after killing the daemon, and suggested using a preemptive registration check as a more robust fix.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, that doesn't matter, it just has to be after the increase of 
queue_refs. I probably missed the complete patch before, but I had
only seen the change in fuse_uring_create(), which is before increase
of queue_refs. 
Code changes in fuse_uring_create_ring_ent() are little bit smaller,
as it already has the free of ring_ent. In the end I don't care and
either way works.

---

I have this in my mind


core-A                                                  core-B

fuse_uring_create_ring_ent()

       ent->headers = headers->iov_base;
       ent->payload = payload->iov_base;
 }
                                             fuse_conn_destroy()
                                                fuse_abort_conn()
                                                  fuse_uring_abort() -> no-op
                                                fuse_wait_aborted()

        atomic_inc(&ring->queue_refs);
                                                fuse_uring_wait_stopped_queues()



Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that a race condition exists between sqe registration and abort logic, agreeing that the preemptive registration check is necessary to prevent hang

Author acknowledged that a wake-up is necessary in the scenario described by Bernd, agreed to modify the patch to include a wake-up when queue_refs hits 0
reviews.raw_body:
I think this is necessary else there is the race where this is the
first sqe that is being registered and it will trigger ring creation
but the ring hasn't been created yet when the abort logic runs. The
fuse_uring_abort() will see that the ring is NULL and skip the call to
fuse_uring_stop_queues() which will skip scheduling the async teardown
worker. Then when the sqe triggers ring creation and later ent
creation and grabs the queue_ref, it can lead to the same hang as the
registration vs. abort one where fuse_wait_aborted() sleeps forever
waiting in queue_refs == 0, if the fuse_wait_aborted() logic runs
between the ent grabbing the queue ref and decrementing it in the if
(!fc->connected) logic.

---

Ah yeah that's a good point, the wakeup would be necessary for this
scenario. I think the atomic_dec() should be changed to

 if (atomic_dec_and_test(&ring->queue_refs))
      wake_up_all(&ring->stop_waitq);

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-14.patch_summary: This patch addresses a potential memory leak and hang issue in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module. The original fix attempted to address a memory leak by freeing up resources, but it did not resolve a registration vs abort race condition that can cause the umount thread to hang indefinitely. This new patch introduces a preemptive registration check to prevent both the memory leak and the hang issue, providing a more robust solution.
